evals.prompt: I need to add gRPC health checking to our Go service. Right now it's just HTTP endpoints but we need proper gRPC health checks for k8s readiness probes, plus graceful shutdown that drains connections. The service is in cmd/server/ and uses google.golang.org/grpc already.
evals.expected_output: Claude should create an ExecPlan at .agents/plans/ with all mandatory sections. The plan should be self-contained and novice-accessible.

assertions.text: An ExecPlan document was created as a markdown file
The plan contains a Purpose or Big Picture section
The plan contains a Progress section with checkboxes
The plan contains a Context and Orientation section
The plan contains a Concrete Steps section with commands
The plan contains a Validation and Acceptance section
The plan has Surprises & Discoveries, Decision Log, and Outcomes & Retrospective
The plan specifically addresses gRPC health checking
The plan addresses graceful shutdown
The plan embeds all needed knowledge, no external references

evals.prompt: refactor the auth middleware to support both JWT and API key auth. currently it only does JWT via the middleware in pkg/auth/jwt.go. API keys should be checked against a postgres table. need to update all the routes in cmd/api/routes.go too
evals.expected_output: Claude should create an ExecPlan covering auth abstraction, database schema, migration, and route updates.

assertions.text: An ExecPlan document was created as a markdown file
The plan contains a Purpose or Big Picture section
The plan contains a Progress section with checkboxes
The plan contains a Context and Orientation section
The plan contains a Concrete Steps section with commands
The plan contains a Validation and Acceptance section
The plan has Surprises & Discoveries, Decision Log, and Outcomes & Retrospective
The plan addresses both JWT and API key auth
The plan mentions postgres/database for API keys
The plan addresses updating routes
The plan embeds all needed knowledge, no external references

evals.prompt: fix the typo in the README where it says 'recieve' instead of 'receive'
evals.expected_output: Claude should NOT create an ExecPlan. It should directly address the typo fix.

assertions.text: No ExecPlan was created — treated as a trivial fix
The response directly addresses the typo fix
No ExecPlan sections like Decision Log or Surprises & Discoveries
